articles.title: House, Senate race to patch surveillance law after GOP revolt, with Trump pressing for a longer extension
articles.summary: A late-night collapse in the House left lawmakers scrambling to keep Section 702 alive, exposing fractures inside the GOP and setting up another fight over privacy, power and national security.

articles.title: IMF warns Middle East war could stall disinflation and keep global growth below trend
articles.summary: The fund’s April outlook says a limited conflict in the Middle East is already enough to lift inflation, tighten financial conditions and slow the world economy after a year of resilience under tariffs.

articles.title: Mars organics, revisited: NASA study keeps the search for life alive
articles.summary: A new analysis of Curiosity data says non-biological chemistry still struggles to explain some of the largest organic molecules ever detected on Mars, sharpening the case for a future sample return.

articles.title: Cannes 2026 leans arthouse as festival confirms a star-sparse competition lineup
articles.summary: The 79th Cannes Film Festival has unveiled a competition slate led by Pedro Almodóvar, Paweł Pawlikowski and Ryusuke Hamaguchi, signaling a more international, director-driven edition as Hollywood stays relatively quiet.

articles.title: OpenAI’s $122 Billion Raise Signals the AI Race Is Now a Capital and Compute War
articles.summary: A fresh OpenAI funding round, a big AWS partnership and rival moves from Microsoft and Google underscore how the AI market in April 2026 is being reshaped by infrastructure, not just models.

articles.title: U.S. chip export reviews are slowing the AI hardware market, and Nvidia feels the drag
articles.summary: A staffing squeeze inside the Commerce Department’s export-control bureau is stretching approval times for AI chip shipments, complicating sales to China and the Middle East.

articles.title: Why 2016 nostalgia is still dominating social feeds in April 2026
articles.summary: A viral throwback trend has turned a decade-old year into a shorthand for pre-pandemic innocence, revealing as much about the present as it does about the past.

articles.title: U.S.-brokered truce in Lebanon tests whether Washington can turn battlefield pause into diplomacy
articles.summary: A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah has eased an explosive front, but the deal’s survival may hinge on whether direct Israel-Lebanon talks can outlast pressure from Hezbollah, Iran and hardliners in Jerusalem.

articles.title: Coachella 2026’s second weekend arrives with pop’s biggest names and a lot to prove
articles.summary: After a blockbuster first weekend, the desert festival’s second act will test whether this year’s mix of star power, surprise collaborations and trend-setting fashion can sustain the momentum.

articles.title: Lebanon truce brings first real test of whether the war can be pulled back from the brink
articles.summary: A fragile 10-day ceasefire is giving displaced Lebanese families a chance to return home, but shelling, battlefield uncertainty and the broader Iran-Israel confrontation are threatening the pause from the start.
